laws.title: Offences in relation to the enemy or terrorist and punishable with death
laws.description: Covers grave offences such as abandoning post, aiding the enemy, cowardice, or communicating with the enemy.
laws.simpleExplanation: If a member helps the enemy, abandons duty, or acts cowardly during operations, the punishment can be death.

punishments.description: Punishable with death or lesser punishment as specified.

laws.title: Offences in relation to the enemy and not punishable with death
laws.description: Covers offences like being captured due to negligence or unauthorized communication with the enemy.
laws.simpleExplanation: If someone is careless or communicates with the enemy without permission, they face serious punishment.

punishments.description: Imprisonment up to fourteen years or lesser punishment.

laws.title: Offences punishable more severely on active duty
laws.description: Includes offences such as leaving post, sleeping on duty, or causing false alarms.
laws.simpleExplanation: Mistakes during active duty like leaving post or sleeping lead to stricter punishment.

punishments.description: Up to fourteen years imprisonment on active duty.
Up to seven years imprisonment when not on active duty.

laws.title: Mutiny
laws.description: Covers mutiny, conspiracy to mutiny, or failure to suppress mutiny.
laws.simpleExplanation: Rebellion or helping rebellion in the force is a very serious crime.

punishments.description: Punishable with death or lesser punishment.

laws.title: Desertion and aiding desertion
laws.description: Covers desertion or attempt to desert and aiding deserters.
laws.simpleExplanation: Leaving duty or helping someone escape duty is punishable.

punishments.description: Punishable with death when on active duty.
Up to seven years imprisonment otherwise.

laws.title: Absence without leave
laws.description: Covers unauthorized absence, overstaying leave, or failure to report for duty.
laws.simpleExplanation: Being absent without permission or not returning on time leads to punishment.

laws.title: Striking or threatening superior officer
laws.description: Covers assault, threat, or insubordinate language toward superior officers.
laws.simpleExplanation: Attacking or threatening a superior officer is a serious offence.

punishments.description: Up to fourteen years imprisonment on active duty.
Up to five years imprisonment otherwise.
